_doc: Runtime profiles. Hermes reads this at the top of each cycle (git pull -> load active_profile). Flip 'active_profile' to switch the whole system between bare Hermes and full orchestration. Edit in GitHub web UI or via the controlplane dashboard; both machines pick up the change on next cycle. Models reference providers in architecture-schema.json; keys resolve via .env / Secrets Manager (never stored here).

default.description: Bare Hermes. Single model, no tiers, no gates, no benchmark logging. Fast and cheap for trivial work or when you just want a straight agent loop.

orchestration.description: Full tier system: planner complexity scoring, gates A-H, micro-orchestrator, verifier, benchmark logging, and the Architect self-tuning loop.
orchestration._compression_note: Leave OFF until baseline numbers exist; enable one layer at a time per benchmark-template.md A/B protocol.

openrouter_only.description: Cheap-to-stand-up testing profile. Everything routes through OpenRouter (one key). Higher per-token cost, but zero direct-key setup — use it to get the pipeline working, then swap to the 'orchestration' profile as you add direct keys.
